# CrudApp - Perl JSON API Framework

> CrudApp is a minimal Perl framework for building JSON REST APIs with authentication and per-user access control. It's designed for shared hosting environments with minimal dependencies.

## Quick Start

To build a Vue.js app with a CrudApp backend:

1. Create your API module (MyApi.pm)
2. Set up the database schema
3. Create users with access rules
4. Build your Vue frontend using the JavaScript client

## Architecture

```
Frontend (Vue.js)  <-->  CrudApp API (Perl CGI)  <-->  MySQL Database
     |                         |
     |                         |
crud-client.js            MyApi.pm extends CrudApp.pm
```

## Backend Setup

### 1. Database Schema

CrudApp requires two system tables for authentication:

```sql
CREATE TABLE _auth (
    id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    username VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    password_salt VARCHAR(32) NOT NULL,
    password_hash VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL,
    access_rules TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE _sessions (
    id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    token VARCHAR(64) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    user_id INT NOT NULL,
    expires_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES _auth(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
);
```

Add your application tables as needed:

```sql
CREATE TABLE todos (
    id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INT NOT NULL,
    title VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    done TINYINT(1) DEFAULT 0,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES _auth(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
);
```

### 2. API Module (MyApi.pm)

```perl
package MyApi;
use strict;
use warnings;
use base 'CrudApp';

sub configure {
    my $app = shift;
    $app->set_db('database_name', 'username', 'password');
}

# Expose tables via CRUD endpoints
sub todos { shift->crud('todos') }
sub posts { shift->crud('posts') }

# Optional: Allow anonymous access to certain tables
sub anon_access {
    return {
        posts => ['read']  # Anyone can read posts
    };
}

# Custom endpoint example
sub dashboard {
    my $app = shift;
    my $user = $app->current_user;
    return $app->render_error("Unauthorized", 401) unless $user;

    $app->render_json({
        total => $app->query_value(
            "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM todos WHERE user_id = ?",
            $user->{id}
        ),
        completed => $app->query_value(
            "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM todos WHERE user_id = ? AND done = 1",
            $user->{id}
        )
    });
}

1;
```

### 3. CGI Entry Point (api.cgi)

```perl
#!/usr/bin/perl
use strict;
use warnings;
use lib '.', '..';
use MyApi;

MyApi->new->run;
```

### 4. Apache .htaccess

```apache
RewriteEngine On

# CRITICAL: Pass Authorization header to CGI
RewriteCond %{HTTP:Authorization} .
RewriteRule .* - [E=HTTP_AUTHORIZATION:%{HTTP:Authorization}]

RewriteBase /

# Serve static files directly
RewriteCond %{REQUEST_FILENAME} -f [OR]
RewriteCond %{REQUEST_FILENAME} -d
RewriteRule ^ - [L]

# Route API requests to CGI
RewriteRule ^(.*)$ api.cgi/$1 [L,QSA]
```

### 5. User Access Rules

Users are created with access_rules JSON that defines what they can do:

```json
{
  "tables": {
    "todos": {
      "access": ["create", "read", "update", "delete"],
      "filters": {"user_id": "$user.id"}
    }
  }
}
```

The filter `{"user_id": "$user.id"}` ensures users only see their own records.

Create users with the create_user.pl script:

```bash
perl create_user.pl username password
```

## Frontend Setup (Vue.js)

### JavaScript Client (crud-client.js)

Include the CrudClient class in your app:

```javascript
class CrudClient {
    constructor(baseUrl, options = {}) {
        this.baseUrl = baseUrl.replace(/\/$/, '');
        this.token = options.token || localStorage.getItem('crud_token');
    }

    setToken(token) {
        this.token = token;
        if (token) {
            localStorage.setItem('crud_token', token);
        } else {
            localStorage.removeItem('crud_token');
        }
    }

    async request(method, path, data = null) {
        const url = `${this.baseUrl}/${path}`;
        const options = {
            method,
            headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }
        };

        if (this.token) {
            options.headers['Authorization'] = `Bearer ${this.token}`;
        }

        if (data && method !== 'GET') {
            options.body = JSON.stringify(data);
        }

        const response = await fetch(url, options);
        const json = await response.json();

        if (!response.ok) {
            const error = new Error(json.error || `HTTP ${response.status}`);
            error.status = response.status;
            throw error;
        }

        return json;
    }

    async login(username, password) {
        const result = await this.request('POST', 'login', { username, password });
        this.setToken(result.token);
        return result;
    }

    async logout() {
        try { await this.request('POST', 'logout'); } catch (e) {}
        this.setToken(null);
    }

    isLoggedIn() {
        return !!this.token;
    }

    table(name) {
        const client = this;
        return {
            list: (params = {}) => {
                const query = new URLSearchParams(params).toString();
                return client.request('GET', query ? `${name}?${query}` : name);
            },
            get: (id) => client.request('GET', `${name}/${id}`),
            create: (data) => client.request('POST', name, data),
            update: (id, data) => client.request('POST', name, { id, ...data }),
            delete: (id) => client.request('DELETE', `${name}/${id}`)
        };
    }
}
```

### Vue.js App Example

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <title>Todo App</title>
    <script src="https://unpkg.com/vue@3/dist/vue.global.prod.js"></script>
</head>
<body>
    <div id="app">
        <!-- Login Form -->
        <div v-if="!token">
            <h1>Login</h1>
            <form @submit.prevent="login">
                <input v-model="username" placeholder="Username" required>
                <input v-model="password" type="password" placeholder="Password" required>
                <button type="submit">Login</button>
            </form>
            <p v-if="error">{{ error }}</p>
        </div>

        <!-- Todo List -->
        <div v-else>
            <h1>My Todos</h1>
            <button @click="logout">Logout</button>

            <form @submit.prevent="addTodo">
                <input v-model="newTitle" placeholder="New todo" required>
                <button type="submit">Add</button>
            </form>

            <ul>
                <li v-for="todo in todos" :key="todo.id">
                    <input type="checkbox" :checked="todo.done" @change="toggleTodo(todo)">
                    <span :style="{ textDecoration: todo.done ? 'line-through' : 'none' }">
                        {{ todo.title }}
                    </span>
                    <button @click="deleteTodo(todo)">Delete</button>
                </li>
            </ul>
        </div>
    </div>

    <script src="js/crud-client.js"></script>
    <script>
        const { createApp, ref, onMounted } = Vue;

        createApp({
            setup() {
                const api = new CrudClient('.');
                const token = ref(localStorage.getItem('crud_token') || '');
                const username = ref('');
                const password = ref('');
                const todos = ref([]);
                const newTitle = ref('');
                const error = ref('');

                if (token.value) api.setToken(token.value);

                async function login() {
                    try {
                        await api.login(username.value, password.value);
                        token.value = api.token;
                        await loadTodos();
                    } catch (e) {
                        error.value = e.message;
                    }
                }

                function logout() {
                    api.logout();
                    token.value = '';
                    todos.value = [];
                }

                async function loadTodos() {
                    const result = await api.table('todos').list();
                    todos.value = result.data || [];
                }

                async function addTodo() {
                    const todo = await api.table('todos').create({ title: newTitle.value });
                    todos.value.push(todo);
                    newTitle.value = '';
                }

                async function toggleTodo(todo) {
                    const updated = await api.table('todos').update(todo.id, {
                        done: todo.done ? 0 : 1
                    });
                    const idx = todos.value.findIndex(t => t.id === todo.id);
                    if (idx >= 0) todos.value[idx] = updated;
                }

                async function deleteTodo(todo) {
                    await api.table('todos').delete(todo.id);
                    todos.value = todos.value.filter(t => t.id !== todo.id);
                }

                onMounted(() => {
                    if (token.value) loadTodos();
                });

                return {
                    token, username, password, todos, newTitle, error,
                    login, logout, addTodo, toggleTodo, deleteTodo
                };
            }
        }).mount('#app');
    </script>
</body>
</html>
```

## API Reference

### Authentication Endpoints

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| POST | /login | Login with `{username, password}`, returns `{token, expires}` |
| POST | /logout | Invalidate current token |

### CRUD Endpoints

For each table exposed via `sub tablename { shift->crud('tablename') }`:

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | /tablename | List records (with user's filters applied) |
| GET | /tablename/:id | Get single record |
| POST | /tablename | Create record (body without id) or update (body with id) |
| DELETE | /tablename/:id | Delete record |

### Response Format

Success responses:
```json
// List
{"data": [...], "limit": 20, "offset": 0}

// Single record
{"id": 1, "title": "...", ...}

// Delete
{"deleted": 1}
```

Error responses:
```json
{"error": "Error message"}
```

## Custom Queries

For joins, aggregations, or complex queries:

```perl
# Single row
my $row = $app->query_row("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?", $id);

# Multiple rows
my $rows = $app->query_rows(
    "SELECT t.*, u.username FROM todos t JOIN _auth u ON u.id = t.user_id"
);

# Single value
my $count = $app->query_value("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM todos WHERE user_id = ?", $uid);

# Execute non-SELECT
$app->query_do("UPDATE todos SET done = 1 WHERE id = ?", $id);
```

## CLI (throwback)

The `throwback` CLI scaffolds projects, initializes SQLite databases, and runs a local dev server.

### Install

```bash
make install
# or: make install PREFIX=/custom/path
```

### Create a New Project

```bash
throwback create myapp
cd myapp
throwback initdb
throwback run
# Open http://localhost:3000/ — login: demo / demo123
```

`throwback create` generates:

```
myapp/
├── MyApp.pm              # API module
├── api.cgi               # CGI entry point
├── .htaccess             # Apache routing (for production)
├── index.html            # Starter page with login
├── js/crud-client.js     # JavaScript API client
├── schema.sql            # MySQL schema (production)
├── schema_sqlite.sql     # SQLite schema (local dev)
├── crudapp.conf          # Dev server config
├── .throwback            # Framework path marker
└── .gitignore
```

### Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `throwback create <dir>` | Scaffold a new project (creates git repo) |
| `throwback initdb` | Create SQLite DB from schema_sqlite.sql, add demo user |
| `throwback run` | Start HTTP server on localhost:3000 |
| `throwback deploy` | Print production deployment steps |

### Adding Tables

1. Add your table to both `schema.sql` (MySQL) and `schema_sqlite.sql` (SQLite)
2. Expose in your API module: `sub posts { shift->crud('posts') }`
3. Set access rules for users (via the _auth table's access_rules JSON)
4. Re-run `throwback initdb` (or delete dev.db and re-run)

### Configuration (crudapp.conf)

```ini
[database]
type = sqlite
path = ./dev.db

[server]
port = 3000
static_dir = .
```

Environment variables override config file values:

```bash
CRUDAPP_DB_TYPE=sqlite CRUDAPP_DB_PATH=./dev.db CRUDAPP_PORT=8080
```

### Local Dev Dependencies

- DBD::SQLite
- HTTP::Daemon (usually pre-installed)

## Dependencies

**Required (all standard or widely available):**
- Perl 5.10+
- CGI (core)
- DBI
- DBD::mysql (production) or DBD::SQLite (local dev)
- JSON
- Digest::SHA (core since 5.10)

**For local development:**
- DBD::SQLite
- HTTP::Daemon

## File Structure

### Framework (this repo)

```
throwback/
├── bin/throwback            # CLI tool
├── CrudApp.pm               # Main framework
├── CrudApp/
│   ├── Config.pm            # Configuration loader
│   ├── DB.pm                # Database adapter factory
│   ├── DevServer.pm         # Local HTTP server module
│   └── DB/
│       ├── MySQL.pm         # MySQL adapter (production)
│       └── SQLite.pm        # SQLite adapter (local dev)
├── example/                 # Example todo app
│   ├── MyApi.pm
│   ├── api.cgi
│   ├── .htaccess
│   ├── index.html
│   ├── js/crud-client.js
│   └── schema.sql
└── llms.txt                 # This documentation
```

### Generated project (from `throwback create`)

```
myapp/
├── MyApp.pm             # API module
├── api.cgi              # CGI entry point
├── .htaccess            # Apache routing (production)
├── index.html           # Starter frontend
├── js/crud-client.js    # JavaScript API client
├── schema.sql           # MySQL schema (production)
├── schema_sqlite.sql    # SQLite schema (local dev)
├── crudapp.conf         # Dev server config
├── .throwback           # Path to framework
└── .gitignore
```

## Common Patterns

### Protected endpoint requiring authentication

```perl
sub my_endpoint {
    my $app = shift;
    my $user = $app->current_user;
    return $app->render_error("Unauthorized", 401) unless $user;

    # ... do work ...
    $app->render_json({ result => 'ok' });
}
```

### Custom endpoint with parameters

```perl
sub search {
    my $app = shift;
    my $q = $app->param('q') or return $app->render_error("Query required", 400);

    my $results = $app->query_rows(
        "SELECT id, title FROM posts WHERE title LIKE ? LIMIT 20",
        "%$q%"
    );
    $app->render_json({ data => $results });
}
```

### Accessing from Vue

```javascript
// Custom endpoint
const stats = await api.request('GET', 'dashboard');
const results = await api.request('GET', 'search?q=hello');

// With POST data
const result = await api.request('POST', 'custom-action', { foo: 'bar' });
```
